threat_model: Trusted capture evidence can accidentally become live screen, camera, microphone, video, audio, sensor, file-write, connector, external-write, raw-media retention, or publication authority if evidence and authority boundaries are not explicit. The mitigation is a digest-only schema and validator that reject raw storage and deny all capture authority in Foundation Mode.

required_checks.check: trusted capture packet live capture, recording, camera, microphone, sensor, connector, write, publication, and terminal authority remain false
redaction check confirms capture evidence remains digest refs and raw surface, media, audio, sensor, source body, and secret storage remain denied
IDOR check confirms tenant scope stays foundation-local-only and capture mode remains dry-run operator-supplied digest
receipt integrity check confirms capture policy, evidence classification, browser observation, UAO, and LifeMeaningJudgment refs are retained
audit visibility check confirms the proof coverage matrix includes the trusted capture evidence packet surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode packet blocks live capture, media recording, screen recording, microphone capture, camera capture, sensor reads, file writes, connector calls, external writes, publication, terminal closure, and success claims.
Foundation Mode packet requires digest refs, raw surface/media/audio/sensor/source-body/secret retention denial, private payload redaction, and operator review.

mitigations: keep TrustedCaptureEvidencePacket validation non-live and deterministic
require digest refs instead of raw browser, screen, video, audio, sensor, source body, or connector payloads
require capture policy, evidence classification, browser observation, UAO, and LifeMeaningJudgment refs
deny live capture, recording, camera, microphone, sensor reads, writes, connector calls, and external writes
deny publication, terminal closure, and success claims in Foundation Mode
